gaps.message: The PRD defines route groups and permissions but lacks a formal sitemap section.
gaps.compensation_hint: Define the screens and routes in the implementation plan based on the route groups and permissions matrix.

gaps.message: The `time_slots` model table in Section 6 does not list `organization_id`, but Section 4 states it is scoped by `organization_id`.
gaps.compensation_hint: Add `organization_id` to the `time_slots` model and migration to ensure robust multi-tenant scoping and indexing.

role_registry.name: Owner
role_registry.description: Full access to everything including billing and user management

role_registry.name: Admin
role_registry.description: Operational admin. Full CRUD on all resources

role_registry.name: Staff
role_registry.description: Can manage availability and update booking status, cannot delete services

role_registry.name: Customer
role_registry.description: Can browse active services and time slots, create and manage own bookings

coverage_matrix.notes: Implemented via two route groups: /api/:organization/ and /api/customer/
Implemented via services model with status active/draft/inactive
Implemented via time_slots model with available boolean
Implemented via bookings model referencing time_slot
Implemented via bookings status transitions and policy checks
Enforced via organization_id scoping and current_user scoping
Enforced via ServiceScope and TimeSlotScope on customer route

hints_for_planner: Add organization_id to time_slots model to satisfy multi-tenant scoping.
Implement custom scopes for Service, TimeSlot, and Booking to handle customer-route filtering.
Ensure customer route uses current_user scoping for bookings.
